item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded:
I just have done a great deal of research on this topic, as its one of the main reasons I joined the foundation.

Thank you for excepting me into the foundation.

Obligatory note, the SCP wiki is not a roleplaying site. When you obtain membership to the wiki, you're not actually part of the fictional Foundation; site members are expected to interact with the forums with that in mind.

item.title: Site Criticism Team Staff Post - Info
item.content:encoded:
Sasboss,

Riemann is correct in that the SCP-Wiki does not have a universal canon. The entire purpose of this site is to explore various fictional writing elements in a collaborative way and each reader is allowed to interpret things however they want.

Even if Clef dropped in to state what his intent was when writing this article, that wouldn't prevent other people from deciding for themselves where this fits into their own individual and unique headcanon.

I realize that you are obviously personally invested in your own particular headcanon, but spam-posting on articles demanding that other people accept your own view is not only rude but rulebreaking. You are welcome to edit your own posts if you have more thoughts to add, but please refrain from replying to your own posts like this in the future.

item.title: Scarlet king.
item.content:encoded: I have a great interest in the Scarlet king, so if you tell me where I can find your mythos, I would gladly read them.

item.title: Actually,
item.content:encoded: I think it fit rather well into the lore of the Scarlet king, though I dont believe it is him. Sure, the formats kinda screwy, but looking past that, I think its a well thought out scp.

item.title: Re: Scarlet King?
item.content:encoded:
True enough, I apologize. I just have done a great deal of research on this topic, as its one of the main reasons I joined the foundation. I will just post that information here, and let people decide for themselves. In "The Door To Another World," the entity inside was hinted at being the Scarlet king, but I personally didn't believe that. After extensive research, I found a match for another entity that could was a match for the Destroyer of Worlds. LORD GORAN, The Horned Brute, The Breaker of Kingdoms, Wrath's Flame, Bane of Angels, the Scarlet King's Sixth Hand. I descovered him while snooping in the Serpent's hand database, and it referances him being of "Massive size" and conquered 48 realms. Then, "At an unknown time, Lord Goran became imprisoned when attempting to conquer a universe already subjugated by an Archon of Yaldabaoth.1011 This enraged the Archon who battled and defeated Lord Goran after an hour. The Archon brought Goran by the horns to the Scarlet King, and demanded why his lord attempted to violate the Treaty of Thorns and Flesh.
The Scarlet King apologized and explained Goran acted alone. To make amends, he punished Lord Goran by banishing him to an unknown plane of existence of little interest" The serpent's hand does not know where he might be imprisoned, but I think I do. It aslo state that Goran will be imprisoned until "The Scarlet King needs him again" The dorr to another world references a small salt plane and at the middle, a massive entity is contained with chains, all but one broken. The pieces fit. I belive the mystery is solved. Thank you for excepting me into the foundation.

item.title: Re: Scarlet King?
item.content:encoded: Hey buddy - glad you're getting into the site and its lore, but remember, canons change from person to person and what I believe might be true can be pretty different from what other people believe! Maybe this is the Scarlet King, the only person that can say is the reader and what they believe.

item.title: Scarlet King?
item.content:encoded: I would like to mention to everyone here that the entity is Not the Scarlet king. If you would like more info on that: contact me, or read my introduction on the new writers thread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is this guy usually drawn with a single cyclopean eye in fanart? I can't find a line mentioning the number of eyes anywhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I could upvote this, I would. Headcanoned.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is my favourite SCP. It scares me the most, it's terrifying. I also love the twist that there's a huge threat that people don't know about, gives me chills +1

item.title: Object Class
item.content:encoded:
I've got to say, reading this after a couple of years really makes you realize how much things have changed. It's a really good article, truly it is, it brought me that sort of terror that I had when I first started reading SCP's. But a change that completely took me out was the re-designation of the final iteration.

CODE NIGHTMARE REGENT RED

Man, I realize that Apollyon was looked down upon, and having it just inspired copy cats, but this just killed the entire vibe for me. It sounds goofy at best, just doesn't feel like it belongs in the article. Maybe that's nostalgia talking, but Apollyon was pretty good, but I understand the change in designation, just something other than that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Noticed a bolding issue on the third tab:

YOU ARE ATTEMPTING TO ACCESS A SECURITY LEVEL 2 (RESTRICTED) FILE.

"security level 2 (restricted)" should be bolded, if it's supposed to fit with the other tabs. I would make the change myself, but the page is locked :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe Clef retroactively altered the class because of the popularity of Apollyon. It was originally supposed to be a throw-away esoteric to inspire fear/peril, but its prevalence in community discussion robbed it of that effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wasn't the class Apollyon before? Why was that changed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One thing I love about this is the increasingly strict security protocol.
Level 0 personnel have a keyphrase/password.
Level 1 personnel have a keyphrase that needs to be put in twice, and a warning for impersonators.
Level 2 personnel have all that and an automated report to RAISA.
Level 3 personnel have all that, a personal access code and a warning of being amnesticized/fired.
Level 4 personnel have all that, a retinal scan and a warning of termination.
O5 personnel have two keyphrases, an automated lethal cognitohazard, an automated MTF summoning order and a warning of immediate termination (plus the RAISA report).
I just love this kinda stuff okay?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, this never happened. I have no clue why you think this is the case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last stage was initially "Apollyon." This new class was specifically deployed to complete the spiral of dread created by the first 5 stages of SCP-2317. But then other people started using Apollyon, like for SCP-3999, which wasn't too bad. As Series V has gone on, more and more articles have started using it (and other "esoteric" classes), to the point where it has lost all meaning. So the writer abandoned it in protest.

item.title: Grammar error
item.content:encoded: For some reason I am not able to edit, but could someone please fix the grammar mistake in Iteration 5, Addendum 2317-2: History:, end of second paragraph?

item.title: A way to delay the inevitable
item.content:encoded:
I would like to see a test log or footnote that shows the attempted use of SCP-___-j

As a way to increase the time before the devourer arrives.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can't help but wonder, that maybe what happened in scp-2935 was last chain breaking faster than expected.

item.title: Now THIS is some really 'good' stuff
item.content:encoded:
There are two types of SCPs that were really able to scare me a lot.

1) those kinds of SCPs where basically if you mess with them you're permanently screwed (aka 'if you touch it / talk to it / mess with it in any other way you're dead') - stuff like SCP-198, SCP-513 or good old 096.

But this masterpiece, SCP-2317 fits into the second category, and that is…

2) something that cannot be contained and is extremely dangerous at the same time - good examples include SCP-169, the old SCP-1548 (aka the hateful star, which was, unfortunately, deleted for some reason), and yes, the Scarlet King SCP-2317.

If you're wondering, yes, I think that SCP-2317 is the Scarlet King, because I just don't see what else it could be. Plus I absolutely love the idea of SCP-999 fighting against SCP-2317 and also against his evil brother, SCP-682 (if you don't know what I'm talking about, read the tale 'A New Job').

Edit: fixed a spelling error

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know I'm a few years late to the party on this one, but I still really like this concept, that the only thing truly being contained here is ourselves.

Regarding the name of the Devourer: The Erikeshan Mystics determined that the Devourer's true name should be forgotten by history, and they presumably went to some lengths to make that happen. But at some point, the Foundation found out that name, entered it into the database, determined that the ancient Mystics really were right to expunge that name from memory, and then redacted it so that nobody, not even Overseers, could know it.

And that brings up some questions: How did the Foundation obtain that name, when the Erikeshans had made that knowledge difficult to obtain? Is it related to six of the chains being broken already?

Since the name is recorded as being redacted and not expunged, is it actually lurking in the database somewhere, hidden even from the Overseers? If that's the case, it suggests that there is a lingering possibility that knowing that name could at some point become necessary.

And if that name is still hidden in the database, it seems likely that steps were taken to prevent that knowledge from being obtainable any other way. Which means that there is a possibility that someone from outside the Foundation might want that information, and that makes safeguarding that name an absolute priority. This fits in well with the notion that, of all the groups within the Foundation, the most powerful (and likely most ruthless) of them isn't some MTF, isn't the vaunted Antimemetics division, and isn't the O5 Council - it's RAISA.

Lots of untold stories here…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good job DrClef.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What a beautiful bitter ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A unique format, which is something I can get behind since it is used for the proper story progression, and a nice crosslink between SCP-231 (even if it was unintentional).
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude, Thanos is nowhere near this thing's size. Thanos is 3 meters tops, this thing's head passed the Karman line. The more comparable being are the Celestials. Although, I did use 2317 to kill Thanos once....

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has Thanos written all over it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Always great to come back to this one. While Clef's entirely within his rights to remove the Apollyon classification, I'm still gonna miss that two hit punch of Object Class: Apollyon, Containment Procedures: Irrelevant.

Always great to come back to this one. While I get why Clef removed the Apollyon designation - it had taken on a meaning beyond what he originally intended and he felt it changed the meaning of the article - CODE NIGHTMARE REGENT RED doesn't quite have the same gutpunch to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm fairly impressed.

1) The format screw is a nice touch.
2) Although I figured the last tab would be very different from the rest, i still surprised me. That bombshell, man!

However, the idea of "we're screwed and there's nothing we can do about it" thing was a little over the top, the fact that the O5 council was trying to cover it up added a nice bit of humanity to the Foundation.

+1

item.title: I love it
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so I'm not the most experienced guy on here (as obvious on my bar), and I've only scratched the surface of the controversy of the article, but in my opinion, the whole apollyon deal is largely irrelevant. The biggest, most impactful thing to me is how the containment procedures grow more and more detailed and intricate and desperate - only to be thrown away and revealed to be pointless.

Its that kind of delicious irony and deception, and the raw jolt of confusion and FEAR at that revelation, which is why I keep coming back to this site.

Yes one can call it cheesy, campy, cliche or gimmicky, with the format screw being a cheap attention grabber. But honestly with all the skips in the 4000 series which just scream at you "DANGER 05 UBER TOP SECRET ONLY BLAAAAH", its so nice to see something which actually fucking builds up how important it is rather than just beating us over the head with it. I fucking love it.

Tl;dr: Clef you (magnificent) bastard take my +1 and shove it, this was chillingly awesome. Love it so much.

item.title: Neat
item.content:encoded:
Was about to -1 it, then I saw the other variations of it
Pretty neat
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're not alone. I've previously said that it sounds like something a Power Ranger knock-off would use to describe a monster. The alliteration adds the cheese factor. That and CASE NIGHTMARE GREEN still sounds a little silly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of those skips that makes me think that nothing would be worth being an 05. I'm a self serving sociopath, but the burden of knowledge makes the power seem like drops of water pouring through my fingers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The journey is what really makes it for me. I had no idea what I was getting into when I first read it, nothing but a growing sense of doom. Always a fun skip to come back to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a little bummed about the class change, but not just because "new thing bad!"

I'm bummed because now one of the parts in Excerpts From "How To Survive When Reality Doesn't", by Alto Clef doesn't make any sense.

"But then Talloran’s like ‘oh, this reality bender that’s torturing me in a living a hell for all eternity and will do the same to everyone else if it gets free is pretty extra, so I’ll classify it as Apollyon too,” and now literally the entire database is classified as Apollyon!"

Oh well. I can definitely sympathize with the DrClef's frustration. And I definitely don't feel that his decision to change it is "petty".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just wondering… what is it stopping el fundacion from producing some replacement super-humongo steel chains (or a great number of smaller ones) to replace the originals?

DID CRAFT SEVEN CUNNING CHAINS OUT OF THE BONES OF JASPETAR, SEVENTH BRIDE OF THE DREAD DEVOURER, MAY HIS NAME REMAIN FOREVER UNREMEMBERED, AND BOUND HIM WITHIN THE PEARL OF THE FATHER OF OYSTERS, HE WHO DWELLS UNDER THE MIRROR OF SALT IN THE REALM UNDER THE SEA.

Since this is thaumaturgy, the big-ass steel chains are really the least important part, the symbolic foci of the working that actually binds.

The Foundation probably lacks a suitable sacrifice (although there are tales, I think, that address the obvious choice here) and the occult technology to carry out a second binding.

Also, how's the 200km high guy going to fit through there to wreak havoc? :)

See SCP-2317-J.

More seriously though, the door was made by 19th century occultists, thousands of years after it was bound there; however the Devourer is able to escape has nothing to do with the doorway. Presumably it can make its own holes; goes with the whole "god of destruction" balliwick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just wondering… what is it stopping el fundacion from producing some replacement super-humongo steel chains (or a great number of smaller ones) to replace the originals? I mean, I know everything has to go through that doorway, but it should be large enough to permit the transport of sufficient equipment to build a factory, then the power and materials to forge that chains within, shouldn't it? I feel like there's some more anomalous reason preventing it instead.

Also, how's the 200km high guy going to fit through there to wreak havoc? :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What makes that silly but CASE NIGHTMARE GREEN isn't?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
CODE NIGHTMARE REGENT RED

This is a brilliant article, what with the build up and the sense of impending doom, but… what? Code nightmare regent red? I laughed when I read that. It's really silly, almost took me out of the whole article. I could see it as something a teenager would write in their overpowered self-insert fan fic. I guess I should defer to better judgement, but I'm sure I'm not alone in finding it over-the-top.

item.title: Re: A humongus plothole
item.content:encoded: I actually tried it — well, not to every exact backspaces, ^H means backspace btw not literally part of the password — I clocked in at 44 second, and I wrote it while looking at the text. I imagine if I memorized it and wrote it like every other Thursday I could do it in half that time.

item.title: Re: A humongus plothole
item.content:encoded:
MS word tells me that the following:

Now is the time for all good men to come to the aid of their party. I^H Because I would not stop for death he ^H^H^H^H^H^H^H^H^H Death he kindly stopped for me. Red Sky in Morning Sailor take e^H wArning.

is 40 words. 40 words per minute is actually a relatively low wpm. Secretaries and receptionists are expected to be able to type at least 55 to 80 wpm. Some of my friends in elementary school got up to the 90s. My fourth grade teacher's wife clocked in at over 100 wpm.

Also, for things like passwords that are probably entered a fair bit more often than most other phrases, muscle memory helps.

But that's just my explanation.

item.title: A humongus plothole
item.content:encoded: how the hell do you type ot the O-5 password in 60 seconds

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it should still be Apollyon, because the other two don't really count; the 001 proposal, as an 001, is even less canon than there-is-no-canon, and the actual 3999 file that the in-universe Foundation uses is just that part at the end, where it's classified as "Neutralized". The rest of it is what Researcher Talloran wrote (or something), but given the nature of 3999 it's not really possible to compare it to this. I mean, we don't really have any conclusive proof that 3999 ever existed.

Also, Big Red here was the first Apollyon. Changing the object class because others were added later would be like rewriting 087 because of all the other infinite structures that came after it. There are only imitators because this one is the best.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But Clef changing the class simply because two of the most incredible SCPs the site has seen (not sure if they are more Apollyons) decided to use it is uh… don't mean to be disrespectful, but just plain petty.

While I can understand not liking the change, it seems deeply unfair to characterize what was probably just a narrative decision as 'petty'.

Context can (and often does) change the meaning of words. Changing a word in your story to better reflect your intent — because the meaning of that word has now changed given new context (such as the emergence of popular skips that use that word in ways you did not intend) — is not 'petty'. 'Petty' would be demanding that other authors change their work for the sake of yours. This is just exercising authorial discretion (for better or worse).

Again, you're free to think this reduces the quality of the work, but I'd otherwise keep such weirdly personal accusations like this to yourself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps. But Clef changing the class simply because two of the most incredible SCPs the site has seen (not sure if they are more Apollyons) decided to use it is uh… don't mean to be disrespectful, but just plain petty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And to be fair, what if WDB and 3999 IS what happens when the Scarlet King is here? SDLocke's WDB is what happens to the world, and LordStoneFish's 3999 is what happens to people's minds.

Oh whatever, I'm going a bit too far with these connections.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So is the Level 4 File the designation for the other realm, and the O5 file the Scarlet King itself?

Also, I absolutely love this SCP, the slow build up from "Alternate reality" as well as the login stuff, to "Scarlet King is coming to kill you all lmaoo" is great as well

Only thing I'm not amazingly happy about is the change from "Apollyon" to "CODE NIGHTMARE REGENT RED", the latter sounds a bit cheesy, and considering changing it because others have started to use it (after a lot of members decided to say it's "Uncontainable, destructive SCPs that will likely cause an XK-Class EOTW Scenario or something worse within the next century or later period."), even though most of the people who used it, the SCPs done were the best that the site has seen, like When Day Breaks, etc.

item.title: I love this format
item.content:encoded: I have got to say that you did an amazing job with this one. I love the format and how much detail you have put into this. Keep it up! (I know this is way late lol)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Presumably some ancient dead Jew came up with the word, first though. Fun fact: some scholars think that Abbadon like Sheol and Gehenna originally was an actual place name, specifically the pit where Israel burned its garbage! Hence "place of destruction".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, my bad then. I had been under the impression that it had originally been created in an old deleted SCP by a different author and that you had revived and popularized it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I note, with bemusement, that 231 and 2317 do share an interesting meta-legacy. They're both articles where people can't seem to stop arguing over what exactly a particular term used in the article (Procedure 110-Montauk vs. Object Class: Apollyon) is supposed to mean.

At least the discussion over Apollyon is less disgusting than the speculation on 110-Montauk.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I did invent the class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If this really is the author's intent, then it doesn't actually make sense in-universe. If the Foundation believes that this entity literally is the one and only mythological Abaddon, why would they make that name it's object class? Wouldn't that be like giving SCP-076 the object class Able?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I guess I just really can't understand this logic. I mean, there is no canon, right? Can't the author just ignore all those other articles that use the object class and say only his is the only Apollyon in his canon?

I dunno, I kind of feel that expecting no one else would ever use such an evocative and awesome class so that this article could stay unique forever… well, it's wishful thinking. Especially considering that Clef didn't actually invent the class. And there's only like 4 or 5 articles that use the class, so it's not like there was a rush of copycat SCPs after this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a cool one, I like the slow build up in the format as more of the mystery is revealed. When you learn the pillars are actually holding the chains of a demonic behemoth under the ground, that's great and creepy as heck, as well as learning most of those chains are broken already. The reveal about the ritual's sacrificial details are good as well.

But the best part is the end when you learn that all the ritual is a lie, that's a really neat little twist. It creates a great sense of foreboding with the SCP.

Personally I don't mind the format of the article, the new text in each section being coloured makes it easy to skip the bits you've read. I also like the authentic feeling of the various log-ins. And honestly only the hardcore give a damn about what class names, I do agree though that the "code nightmare regent red" does sound a bit cheesy.

The only thing I think doesn't really work is the final section where you learn the back story of the scarlet king. I found the whole old society stuff kinda lame. I think it would be far more interesting and ominous to know much less about it aside from the fact it was imprisoned some time in the past, it's imprisonment is failing and probably inevitable, and the ritual is a lie.

I also found the stuff about a staff member having to ruse as a D-class personnel confusing, and I didn't understand why or what it adds to this SCP (unless it's related to SCP 231, see below).

Overall though this captured my imagination with its horrible reveals as the story escalates.

PS I am a casual SCP partaker. I saw people in the comments mentioning SCP 231 and it being linked in SCP 2317's article. But I never saw any link, was that an older version of the article? I haven't read SCP 231 but if there is supposed to be a reference then the casual user will currently miss it. If it's just for the SCP veterans to know as an easter egg, that's fine.

item.title: Re: Changed a couple of words:
item.content:encoded: Agreed - I loved that, after 5 iterations of the procedures expanding and growing, the succinctness was very ominous and honestly the most amazing part of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I meant they'd have their own object class, not they have their own object class entirely unique to just them and them alone. Bad wording on my part, yeah.

I meant it can be possible all scarlet king SCPs have the Apollyon class from the overseers' perspective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't speak for them, but MarqFJA87 was likely referring to this bit:

This SCP was supposed to be Abaddon (the redacted 4-letter name would be אבדון I believe.) and every SCP would have its own object class that reveals its nature.

The point of a class is classification; if every SCP had its own class, then there's no actual classification going on. For a parallel, imagine that, instead of species, we just assigned every organism as a member of its own isolated species. You can argue that this is a more accurate approach, but it's also the opposite of speciation.

The whole point of taxonomy is to assign members to a group based on a similarity common to its members. If every member is assigned to its own isolated group, you're no longer classifying things. You're just describing them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's wrong? Does "Object Class" means just 3 different "Power Levels" to you? That's ridiculous, and it makes no sense we went with this "Safe Euclid Keter" thing in the first place, with 3 words that have absolutely no meaning to each other.

Object Classes means "biological, mechanical, sentient, noneuclidean, (anti)memetic, pataphysical" to me. Dunno about you. Like how SCPs related to the Angel of Destruction would be labelled as such, and those related to CotBG would have their own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I apologize to anyone who might feel offended by this, but DrClef's response to the community deciding to re-use the object class for other scips strikes me as simply petty.

Besides, think about it: in-universe, it could easily have originally been coined by the O5 as a one-off classification because the Scarlet King (or whichever anomaly that first got the class applied to it) was the first time the Foundation has ever found itself with something that made every other known Keter at the time (and later on, the vast majority of Keter anomalies, including several apocalyptic ones) look insignificant, but then they end up discovering a few more anomalies that convince them to apply the class to them as well.
